summary: PR #2380 linked melee/mn/mngallery.c by marking it Matching, restructuring mngallery source around typed userdata/static model data, adjusting adjacent mn/mnsnap ownership in splits/symbols, and cleaning some literals and local symbols. The bot report recorded mngallery linked-code/data progress and fn_802590C4 reaching 100%, while review discussion focused on direct HSD_GObjGetUserData use versus a file-local GET_804D6C88 helper.

key_files.role: Primary matching source: added typed mngallery userdata structs, local helper macros, static model descriptors, inline literals, function scope/static changes, and matching tactics around fn_802590C4.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: src/melee/mn/mngallery.c include cleanup and StaticModelDesc/mnGallery_804D6C88 declarations
raw_diff: src/melee/mn/mngallery.c adds GET_804D6C88, mnGallery_804D6C88_userdata, mnGallery_child_userdata, STATIC_ASSERTs

key_files.role: Build configuration changed mngallery object from NonMatching to Matching.
Adjusted mnsnap and mngallery bss/data/sdata split boundaries.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mngallery .data end 0x803F06D0 to 0x803F06CC, .bss start 0x804A0B90 to 0x804A0BA0, .sdata end 0x804D50A0 to 0x804D509D

key_files.role: Updated symbol ownership/scope for mngallery local rodata/data/sdata/sdata2 labels and corrected 0x804A0B90 ownership to mnSnap.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnGallery_803F0570/803F0580 expanded into local string/object labels
raw_diff: mnGallery_804D5090/804D5098 and mnGallery_804DC358..804DC390 changed to local labels

key_files.role: Adjusted game-mode allocation code to use mnSnap_804A0B90 and include mnsnap.h.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_801B0FF8 loop changed from mnGallery_804A0B90/struct mnGallery_804A0B90_t to mnSnap_804A0B90/struct mnSnap_804A0B90_t

key_files.role: Header surface changed, but exact declarations are not visible in the loaded diff excerpt.
Header surface changed to support mnsnap symbol ownership, but exact added declaration is not visible in the loaded diff excerpt.
Type definitions changed, but exact type changes are not visible in the loaded diff excerpt.

what_changed.text: mngallery.c was promoted to Matching in configure.py.
The source replaced several ad hoc local/padded structs with a file-local mnGallery_804D6C88_userdata struct, a child userdata struct, GET_* helpers, and STATIC_ASSERT size checks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: adds struct mnGallery_804D6C88_userdata and STATIC_ASSERT size 0x24
raw_diff: adds struct mnGallery_child_userdata and STATIC_ASSERT size 8

what_changed.text: The source removed several extern data references and used inline literals for camera vectors, zero/far values, and movie file names such as "MvOmake15.mth" and "MvHowTo.mth".
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnGallery_80258A08 replaces extern Vec3/floats with Vec3 eye/interest literals and 0.0f/2.0f
raw_diff: mnGallery_80258BC4 replaces mnGallery_803F0570/mnGallery_803F0580 with string literals

what_changed.text: mnsnap/mngallery data ownership was adjusted: split boundaries moved and mnGallery_804A0B90 was renamed/attributed to mnSnap_804A0B90 in symbols and gm_1B03 allocation code.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: splits.txt bss boundary changes for mnsnap and mngallery
raw_diff: gm_1B03.c uses mnSnap_804A0B90 and struct mnSnap_804A0B90_t

what_changed.text: The bot report recorded 7 new matches, including mngallery data/bss/rodata/sdata2 progress and fn_802590C4 reaching 100%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev[bot] report, 7 new matches; fn_802590C4 +2 bytes 99.19% to 100.00%

smart_moves.text: Correcting 0x804A0B90 ownership to mnSnap and updating gm_1B03.c avoided keeping an apparently wrong mngallery symbol for mnsnap allocation data.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1B03.c allocation loop changed to mnSnap_804A0B90 and mnSnap_804A0B90_t

smart_moves.text: Inlining ordinary asset/file strings instead of referencing generated mnGallery data symbols reduced reliance on named data where local source did not require a global symbol.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbMthp_8001F410 now called with "MvOmake15.mth" and "MvHowTo.mth"

smart_moves.text: Introducing userdata structs and STATIC_ASSERTs made repeated raw/padded access shapes more reviewable while preserving known size constraints.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: adds mnGallery_804D6C88_userdata and mnGallery_child_userdata with STATIC_ASSERTs

decomp_lessons.text: When a menu file's data ownership is ambiguous, check adjacent TU boundaries and callsites before accepting address-style names; this PR moved 0x804A0B90 from mngallery to mnsnap and updated the allocation code accordingly.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: splits.txt mnsnap .bss expands to 0x804A0BA0 while mngallery .bss starts at 0x804A0BA0

decomp_lessons.text: For GObj userdata in subsystem-local code, a local GET_* macro that wraps HSD_GObjGetUserData and casts to the recovered userdata type can document the intended access pattern, but matching may still require direct HSD_GObjGetUserData at specific callsites.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: defines GET_804D6C88 as cast of HSD_GObjGetUserData
review_comments.md: jellejurre says HSD_GObjGetUserData had to be used directly instead of helper method
review_comments.md: BR- explains HSD_GObjGetUserData drives GET_FIGHTER and GET_804D6C88 mimics that pattern

decomp_lessons.text: Replacing generated extern literals with source-level literals and ordinary local constants can pair with symbol-scope cleanup when the PR is explicitly linking the object and data labels are adjusted accordingly.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mngallery.c replaces extern vectors/floats/strings with inline literals
raw_diff: symbols.txt converts mngallery rodata/data/sdata/sdata2 globals to local @ labels
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report records mngallery .data/.rodata/.sdata2 progress

naming_conventions.text: Recovered field name: data->unk14 was renamed to data->state where menu state comparisons/assignments are visible.
evidence_refs: raw_diff/review hunk: data->unk14 = 3 changed to data->state = 3

naming_conventions.text: For still-uncertain struct members, the PR retained conservative names such as unk0, unk1, unk2, unk15 while naming clearer pointer roles gobj4, gobj8, sobj, text, frame, and gobjs.
The local userdata accessor name GET_804D6C88 follows the address-based naming style used when semantic meaning is not fully known.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: BR- says GET_804D6C88 is a macro made to mimic GET_FIGHTER for this file

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: The PR removed some optimization pragmas around mngallery functions but retained a narrow #pragma dont_inline around mnGallery_80258D50.
evidence_refs: review hunk: removes #pragma optimization_level 2, opt_strength_reduction on, opt_propagation off before fn_802590C4
raw_diff: #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on around mnGallery_80258D50

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: A direct HSD_GObjGetUserData call was used in fn_802590C4_inline because the file-local GET_804D6C88 helper reportedly broke matching at that site.
evidence_refs: review hunk: tmp = HSD_GObjGetUserData(gobj); /// @todo GET_804D6C88 breaks these

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: The source includes narrow matching hacks/comments in fn_80258ED0_helper, including direct user_data access because GET_804D6C88 'blows the stack' and a volatile cast marked 'hacky'.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80258ED0_helper comments: GET_804D6C88 blows the stack; HSD_GObj* volatile cast marked hacky

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: A small float_order_helper was introduced to set sobj->x10 = 96.0f and sobj->x14 = 72.0f, apparently preserving float/order behavior while avoiding named extern float data.
PAD_STACK(0x10) remains in mnGallery_8025896C, so any reuse of this tactic should require narrow objdiff evidence.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.text: Asked 'what's this?' on the fn_802590C4_inline change that directly called HSD_GObjGetUserData with a TODO about GET_804D6C88 breaking.

review_feedback.author: jellejurre
review_feedback.text: Explained that the noteworthy issue was needing to use HSD_GObjGetUserData directly instead of the helper method defined for the file.

review_feedback.author: BR-
review_feedback.text: Clarified that HSD_GObjGetUserData is the inline behind GET_FIGHTER-style accessors, returns userdata through void*, and that GET_804D6C88 was made to mimic GET_FIGHTER by calling the inline and casting to mnGallery_804D6C88_userdata*.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR state MERGED, changed_files counts
raw_diff: truncated at 34343 chars but includes main hunks
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report and review comments
human_pr_text.md: author note says 90% done and mentions messy mnGallery_t and unknown mnGallery_804D6C88 struct
notes: Loaded raw_diff is truncated, so exact final contents of later mngallery.c hunks, mngallery.h, mnsnap.h, and types.h are not fully visible.
Bot report provides progress/match metrics, but no local build command, objdiff command, or adjacent-function regression evidence is shown in the slice.

The PR author's comment says '90% done' and calls out messy mnGallery_t and unresolved mnGallery_804D6C88 struct understanding; the merge state and bot report indicate accepted progress, but the slice should not be read as proving all struct semantics.
Review discussion documents the HSD_GObjGetUserData/GET_804D6C88 issue, but does not prove a general rule that direct calls are always preferable.

accepted_candidate_records.title: GObj userdata helpers may be useful but matching can require direct HSD_GObjGetUserData at isolated sites
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2380, mngallery introduced GET_804D6C88 as a GET_FIGHTER-like wrapper around HSD_GObjGetUserData, but fn_802590C4_inline used HSD_GObjGetUserData directly with a TODO that GET_804D6C88 broke matching. Review clarified that HSD_GObjGetUserData is the inline behind GET_FIGHTER-style accessors and simply returns userdata through void* before the wrapper casts it.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Check adjacent TU data ownership when linking menu files
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2380 linked mngallery while moving the 0x804A0B90 bss object from mnGallery to mnSnap ownership, expanding the mnsnap bss split and updating gm_1B03 allocation code to include mnsnap.h and use mnSnap_804A0B90.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1B03.c allocation loop uses mnSnap_804A0B90

source_update_candidates.title: mngallery linked as Matching in PR #2380
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2380 changed configure.py so Object(NonMatching, "melee/mn/mngallery.c") became Object(Matching, "melee/mn/mngallery.c"). The bot report recorded linked code +4268 bytes and 7 new matches including mngallery .data/.bss/.rodata/.sdata2 and fn_802590C4.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path fact about object matching status and historical progress; should be checked against current source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: 0x804A0B90 bss ownership moved from mngallery to mnsnap
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2380 changed symbols.txt from mnGallery_804A0B90 to mnSnap_804A0B90, expanded mnsnap .bss to 0x804A0BA0, started mngallery .bss at 0x804A0BA0, and updated gm_1B03.c allocation code to use mnSnap_804A0B90.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: splits.txt mnsnap/mngallery bss boundary changes
source_update_candidates.reason: Concrete historical ownership correction, but current source should verify whether this fact remains accurate.

source_update_candidates.title: GET_* userdata wrappers are not mandatory when they perturb matching
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a note under typed-fields/accessors or matching tactics: file-local GET_* wrappers around HSD_GObjGetUserData can improve readability, but a direct HSD_GObjGetUserData call may be acceptable at narrow sites when the wrapper changes stack/codegen and the reason is documented.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: jellejurre says direct HSD_GObjGetUserData was needed instead of helper
review_comments.md: BR- explains relationship between HSD_GObjGetUserData and GET_FIGHTER-style macros
raw_diff: TODO comments around GET_804D6C88 breaking/blowing stack
source_update_candidates.reason: May refine existing standards on typed fields and matching tactics, but evidence is one PR and should not become a broad exception without curator review.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize that all GET_* macros should be avoided; the review explicitly says GET_804D6C88 mimics GET_FIGHTER and only documents local breakage in this PR.
Do not promote the exact mnGallery_804D6C88_userdata field semantics beyond names shown in the diff; the author explicitly noted uncertainty about the struct.
Do not treat the local labels @144/@157/etc. as durable semantic names; they are symbol/split metadata in the PR evidence, not source-level meaning.
Do not claim full objdiff/regression validation beyond the bot report, because no command output or adjacent regression ledger is included in the slice.
